title: Customer support chat: bidirectional message delivery (dbg143)
description:
After dbg142, Mike and Kat could both see an active chat session and send messages, but only each user's own messages appeared — nothing crossed in real time.

Root cause (dbg143): dbg142 added `if (!isChatComposerLive()) return` on incoming `chat-message` SSE events. That gate was meant for the send composer but silently dropped peer messages whenever live-mode state lagged session status (common on the customer side after accept, or when session IDs differed by case). Own messages still showed via optimistic POST append, which matched the screenshot.

Fix: never gate inbound chat-message on composer live state — always append when sessionId matches, promote widget to live mode, and dedupe by message id. Normalize session UUID comparison (case-insensitive). Poll GET /messages on heartbeat while chat is active as SSE fallback. POST response ids used for optimistic send dedupe. Backend SSE payload sessionId stringified explicitly.

Who is affected: Horizon admins and customers using PipeShare customer support chat on the OVH private server. Deploy both frontend and backend together.
changeLog: Remove composer-live gate on inbound chat-message SSE — peer messages always render when session matches.
Normalize sessionId comparison; stringify sessionId in backend chat-message broadcast.
Dedupe messages by id in widget; optimistic send uses POST response id.
Poll GET /messages on heartbeat during active chat as SSE fallback.
Use live supportUserId() for mine/other styling instead of stale init snapshot.
Cache bust dbg143 (portal runtime + PipeShare addon manifest + PipeSync manifest).
